message:
refactor: websocket hex literals, gf256 pattern collapse, issue tracker sync

- net/websocket/frame.rs: replace magic decimal 126/127 with hex 0x7E/0x7F
  in FrameCodec::encode payload length markers for clarity against the
  WebSocket RFC which uses hex notation

- raptorq/gf256.rs: collapse separate match arms into combined or-patterns
  (Some("off" | "sequential"), Some("fused" | "force_fused")) in
  detect_dual_policy; rename test variables dst_a_fused/dst_b_fused →
  accum_left/accum_right for clearer intent in addmul_slices2 asymmetric
  length test

- .beads/issues.jsonl: sync issue tracker with current bead state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-23kbc","title":"RuntimeState contention + access inventory","description":"Map every hot-path access to RuntimeState fields to identify which data can be sharded.

Scope:
- Worker execution path (polling, wake, stored_futures).
- Scheduler queues and stealing.
- LocalQueue / IntrusiveStack access patterns.
- Cancellation/obligation paths that touch multiple arenas.

Deliverables:
- Table of field access sites with frequency and lock needs.
- Proposed shard boundaries (tasks/regions/obligations/futures/time).
- List of cross-entity operations that require multi-lock access.","notes":"Completed full contention inventory. Deliverable: docs/runtime_state_contention_inventory.md. Key findings: (1) tasks + stored_futures are hottest shard, (2) trace/metrics can be extracted from Mutex (already Arc+atomics), (3) config fields are read-only after init, (4) wake_state dedup checks can be lock-free, (5) task_completed is the bottleneck touching all shards. Proposed 5 shards: A=Tasks, B=Regions, C=Obligations, D=Instrumentation, E=Config.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FuchsiaCliff","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:55:11.920238484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:02:04.022574188Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:02:04.022434519Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23kbc","depends_on_id":"bd-2ijqf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-23lw","title":"Budget monitor: real-time budget consumption across regions","description":"# Budget Monitor

## Overview
Real-time dashboard for monitoring budget consumption across all regions,
with alerts, trends, and exhaustion prediction.

## Design

### Budget Monitor
```rust
pub struct BudgetMonitor {
    state: Arc<RuntimeState>,
    console: Console,
    history: BudgetHistory,
}

impl BudgetMonitor {
    /// Get current budget status for all regions.
    pub fn status(&self) -> Vec<RegionBudgetStatus> {
        self.state.all_regions()
            .map(|r| RegionBudgetStatus {
                region_id: r.id(),
                region_name: r.name(),
                total: r.budget().total(),
                remaining: r.budget().remaining(),
                consumed: r.budget().consumed(),
                percent_used: r.budget().percent_used(),
                exhaustion_eta: self.predict_exhaustion(&r),
            })
            .collect()
    }
    
    /// Predict when a region will exhaust its budget.
    fn predict_exhaustion(&self, region: &Region) -> Option<Duration> {
        let history = self.history.get(region.id());
        let rate = history.consumption_rate()?;
        let remaining = region.budget().remaining();
        
        if rate > 0.0 {
            Some(Duration::from_secs_f64(remaining as f64 / rate))
        } else {
            None
        }
    }
    
    /// Get budget breakdown by resource type.
    pub fn breakdown(&self, region_id: RegionId) -> BudgetBreakdown {
        let region = self.state.get_region(region_id);
        BudgetBreakdown {
            ops: region.budget().ops_consumed(),
            bytes: region.budget().bytes_consumed(),
            time: region.budget().time_consumed(),
            connections: region.budget().connections_consumed(),
        }
    }
}
```

### Output Format
```
Budget Monitor
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Region                     Budget          ETA     Rate        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Root (Region-0)           ████████░░  80%   -       -          │
│ ├─ HttpServer (Region-1)  ███████░░░  70%  12m     58 ops/s    │
│ │  ├─ Request-1           █████░░░░░  50%   5m    120 ops/s    │
│ │  └─ Request-2           ██░░░░░░░░  20%   2m    180 ops/s    │
│ └─ BackgroundJobs         ██████████ 100%   -       -          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ⚠️  ALERTS:                                                     │
│   Request-2 will exhaust budget in ~2m at current rate         │
│   Request-1 consumption rate increasing (+15% last 30s)        │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Budget Breakdown: Request-2 (Region-3)
╭────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Resource          Used    /   Total    │
├────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Operations        8,234   /  10,000    │
│ Bytes            12.3 MB  /  50 MB     │
│ Time              2.1s    /   5s       │
│ Connections          2    /   5        │
╰────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Historical Trends
- Consumption rate over time
- Peak usage identification
- Trend analysis (increasing/decreasing)
- Alerting on anomalies

### Interactive Features (TUI)
- Drill into region breakdown
- Historical graph view
- Alert threshold configuration
- Auto-refresh interval

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Real-time budget status
- [ ] Per-region breakdown
- [ ] Consumption rate tracking
- [ ] Exhaustion prediction
- [ ] Alert system
- [ ] Historical trends
- [ ] TUI dashboard
- [ ] Unit tests

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- `monitor::tests::status_all_regions` - Region status
- `monitor::tests::breakdown_by_type` - Resource breakdown
- `monitor::tests::consumption_rate` - Rate calculation
- `monitor::tests::exhaustion_prediction` - ETA prediction
- `monitor::tests::alert_thresholds` - Alert triggering

### Integration Tests
- `monitor::integration::live_budget_tracking` - Real runtime
- `monitor::integration::rapid_consumption` - Fast budget use

### Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Individual budget queries
- DEBUG: Rate calculations
- INFO: Status queries
- WARN: Budget exhaustion warnings","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:58:37.210950005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:37:12.859001268Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:37:12.858818358Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23lw","depends_on_id":"bd-nugw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:34Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-23lw","depends_on_id":"bd-vpkn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:34Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-sd083","title":"[SPORK] Applications: Start/Stop as Regions (OTP feel, asupersync semantics)","notes":"Closeout validation complete: dependency epics are closed (bd-1vk6c, bd-3rpp8, bd-2n7yw, bd-1zok9, bd-qbcnu). App-layer child slices are closed (bd-1zkki AppSpec/AppHandle, bd-32w45 compile AppSpec->SupervisorSpec/regions, bd-2ukjr registry capability wiring, bd-j1r0f deterministic start/stop ordering contract, bd-21k66 lab AppScenario integration, bd-3mqxu crashpack hook, bd-mo78w minimal supervised app docs example). Acceptance intent for App start/stop as region-owned lifecycle is now represented in landed tasks/tests/docs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"FoggyMarsh","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:41:12.705325957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:01.210125267Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:01.210063342Z","close_reason":"Completed via closed child slices covering AppSpec/AppHandle lifecycle, compilation/wiring, deterministic ordering contract, lab scenario integration, crashpack hook, and docs example.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["app","cancel-correct","determinism","otp","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sd083","depends_on_id":"bd-1jsq9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:44Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-sd083","depends_on_id":"bd-1vk6c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:44Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-sd083","depends_on_id":"bd-1zok9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:44Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-sd083","depends_on_id":"bd-2n7yw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:44Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-sd083","depends_on_id":"bd-3rpp8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:44Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-sd083","depends_on_id":"bd-qbcnu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:44Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1314,"issue_id":"bd-sd083","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Spork "Application" Layer (OTP-style, region-compiled)

OTP's killer feature is not only supervisors; it's that *applications* are the unit of deployment/ops.

Spork needs an explicit application layer so users (and coding agents) can:

- define a top-level tree of supervised services
- start/stop it with deterministic semantics
- get a single handle for shutdown (close => quiescence)
- integrate with lab harness + crash artifacts without extra wiring

## Design: Compile an AppSpec to a Root Region

An application is a *region-owned* tree.

- `AppSpec` defines the topology: supervisors + child specs + registry wiring.
- `App::start(cx)` allocates a root region (or uses an existing one) and boots the tree.
- `AppHandle::stop(cx)` requests cancel on the root region and waits for quiescence.

Key property vs OTP:
- In OTP, a supervision tree can outlive the call site if you're sloppy.
- In Spork, the tree is *owned* by a region and must close to quiescence.

## Deterministic Start/Stop Contracts

We need explicit ordering contracts so that lab replay and DPOR exploration are meaningful:

- deterministic child start order (stable sort key)
- deterministic stop ordering (reverse-topo or spec-defined)
- deterministic DOWN/EXIT delivery ordering during shutdown

## Observability + Agent Workflow

App start/stop must automatically attach:

- trace collection
- crashpack builder hooks
- evidence ledger root (so supervisors can explain restarts)

The goal: a coding agent can run a single scenario and get:
- a stable report fingerprint
- minimal repro artifacts

## Acceptance Criteria

- API supports a minimal `AppSpec` + `AppHandle` path for real apps.
- Integration tests prove:
  - close => quiescence
  - no task leaks / obligation leaks
  - deterministic ordering of start/stop side effects
- Lab harness can run an app spec as a scenario.
","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:43:03Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-sn38","title":"[Stream] Implement Channel-to-Stream Adapters","description":"# Channel-to-Stream Adapters

## Overview
Wrappers that convert channel receivers into streams for pipeline processing.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: ReceiverStream (from mpsc)
```rust
use crate::channel::mpsc;
use pin_project::pin_project;

/// Stream wrapper for mpsc::Receiver
#[pin_project]
pub struct ReceiverStream<T> {
    #[pin]
    inner: mpsc::Receiver<T>,
}

impl<T> ReceiverStream<T> {
    /// Create from receiver
    pub fn new(recv: mpsc::Receiver<T>) -> Self {
        Self { inner: recv }
    }
    
    /// Get reference to inner receiver
    pub fn get_ref(&self) -> &mpsc::Receiver<T> {
        &self.inner
    }
    
    /// Get mutable reference to inner receiver
    pub fn get_mut(&mut self) -> &mut mpsc::Receiver<T> {
        &mut self.inner
    }
    
    /// Unwrap to inner receiver
    pub fn into_inner(self) -> mpsc::Receiver<T> {
        self.inner
    }
    
    /// Close the receiver
    pub fn close(&mut self) {
        self.inner.close()
    }
}

impl<T> Stream for ReceiverStream<T> {
    type Item = T;
    
    fn poll_next(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Option<T>> {
        self.project().inner.poll_recv(cx)
    }
}

impl<T> From<mpsc::Receiver<T>> for ReceiverStream<T> {
    fn from(recv: mpsc::Receiver<T>) -> Self {
        Self::new(recv)
    }
}
```

### Step 2: UnboundedReceiverStream
```rust
/// Stream wrapper for unbounded mpsc::Receiver
#[pin_project]
pub struct UnboundedReceiverStream<T> {
    #[pin]
    inner: mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<T>,
}

impl<T> UnboundedReceiverStream<T> {
    pub fn new(recv: mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<T>) -> Self {
        Self { inner: recv }
    }
    
    pub fn into_inner(self) -> mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<T> {
        self.inner
    }
    
    pub fn close(&mut self) {
        self.inner.close()
    }
}

impl<T> Stream for UnboundedReceiverStream<T> {
    type Item = T;
    
    fn poll_next(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Option<T>> {
        self.project().inner.poll_recv(cx)
    }
}
```

### Step 3: WatchStream (from watch channel)
```rust
use crate::channel::watch;

/// Stream that yields when watch value changes
#[pin_project]
pub struct WatchStream<T> {
    #[pin]
    inner: watch::Receiver<T>,
    /// Track if we've seen the initial value
    has_seen_initial: bool,
}

impl<T: Clone> WatchStream<T> {
    /// Create from watch receiver
    pub fn new(recv: watch::Receiver<T>) -> Self {
        Self {
            inner: recv,
            has_seen_initial: false,
        }
    }
    
    /// Create, skipping the initial value
    pub fn from_changes(recv: watch::Receiver<T>) -> Self {
        let mut stream = Self::new(recv);
        stream.has_seen_initial = true; // Skip initial
        stream
    }
}

impl<T: Clone + Send + Sync> Stream for WatchStream<T> {
    type Item = T;
    
    fn poll_next(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Option<T>> {
        let this = self.project();
        
        // First poll: return current value immediately
        if \!*this.has_seen_initial {
            *this.has_seen_initial = true;
            return Poll::Ready(Some(this.inner.borrow().clone()));
        }
        
        // Wait for changes
        match this.inner.poll_changed(cx) {
            Poll::Ready(Ok(())) => {
                Poll::Ready(Some(this.inner.borrow().clone()))
            }
            Poll::Ready(Err(_)) => {
                // Sender dropped
                Poll::Ready(None)
            }
            Poll::Pending => Poll::Pending,
        }
    }
}
```

### Step 4: BroadcastStream
```rust
use crate::channel::broadcast;

/// Stream wrapper for broadcast receiver
#[pin_project]
pub struct BroadcastStream<T> {
    #[pin]
    inner: broadcast::Receiver<T>,
}

impl<T: Clone> BroadcastStream<T> {
    pub fn new(recv: broadcast::Receiver<T>) -> Self {
        Self { inner: recv }
    }
}

/// Error from broadcast stream
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum BroadcastStreamRecvError {
    /// Lagged behind, some messages missed
    Lagged(u64),
}

impl<T: Clone + Send> Stream for BroadcastStream<T> {
    type Item = Result<T, BroadcastStreamRecvError>;
    
    fn poll_next(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Option<Self::Item>> {
        let this = self.project();
        
        match this.inner.poll_recv(cx) {
            Poll::Ready(Ok(item)) => Poll::Ready(Some(Ok(item))),
            Poll::Ready(Err(broadcast::RecvError::Lagged(n))) => {
                Poll::Ready(Some(Err(BroadcastStreamRecvError::Lagged(n))))
            }
            Poll::Ready(Err(broadcast::RecvError::Closed)) => {
                Poll::Ready(None)
            }
            Poll::Pending => Poll::Pending,
        }
    }
}
```

### Step 5: Stream Sink Helpers
```rust
/// Convert a stream into a channel sender
pub fn into_sink<T>(sender: mpsc::Sender<T>) -> SinkStream<T> {
    SinkStream { sender }
}

/// Sink wrapper for mpsc sender
pub struct SinkStream<T> {
    sender: mpsc::Sender<T>,
}

impl<T> SinkStream<T> {
    /// Send item through the channel
    pub async fn send(&self, item: T) -> Result<(), SendError<T>> {
        self.sender.send(item).await
    }
    
    /// Send all items from stream
    pub async fn send_all<S>(&self, mut stream: S) -> Result<(), SendError<S::Item>>
    where
        S: Stream<Item = T> + Unpin,
    {
        while let Some(item) = stream.next().await {
            self.sender.send(item).await?;
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}

/// Forward stream to channel
pub async fn forward<S, T>(
    mut stream: S,
    sender: mpsc::Sender<T>,
) -> Result<(), SendError<T>>
where
    S: Stream<Item = T> + Unpin,
{
    while let Some(item) = stream.next().await {
        sender.send(item).await?;
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

## Testing

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_receiver_stream() {
    let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(10);
    let mut stream = ReceiverStream::new(rx);
    
    tx.send(1).await.unwrap();
    tx.send(2).await.unwrap();
    tx.send(3).await.unwrap();
    drop(tx);
    
    assert_eq\!(stream.next().await, Some(1));
    assert_eq\!(stream.next().await, Some(2));
    assert_eq\!(stream.next().await, Some(3));
    assert_eq\!(stream.next().await, None);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_receiver_stream_collect() {
    let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(10);
    
    // Send in background
    tokio::spawn(async move {
        for i in 1..=5 {
            tx.send(i).await.unwrap();
        }
    });
    
    let stream = ReceiverStream::new(rx);
    let collected: Vec<_> = stream.collect().await;
    
    assert_eq\!(collected, vec\![1, 2, 3, 4, 5]);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_watch_stream() {
    let (tx, rx) = watch::channel(0);
    let mut stream = WatchStream::new(rx);
    
    // Initial value
    assert_eq\!(stream.next().await, Some(0));
    
    // Update value
    tx.send(1).unwrap();
    assert_eq\!(stream.next().await, Some(1));
    
    tx.send(2).unwrap();
    assert_eq\!(stream.next().await, Some(2));
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_watch_stream_from_changes() {
    let (tx, rx) = watch::channel(0);
    let mut stream = WatchStream::from_changes(rx);
    
    // Should skip initial value
    tx.send(1).unwrap();
    assert_eq\!(stream.next().await, Some(1));
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_broadcast_stream() {
    let (tx, rx) = broadcast::channel(10);
    let mut stream = BroadcastStream::new(rx);
    
    tx.send(1).unwrap();
    tx.send(2).unwrap();
    
    assert_eq\!(stream.next().await.unwrap().unwrap(), 1);
    assert_eq\!(stream.next().await.unwrap().unwrap(), 2);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_forward() {
    let (tx_out, rx_out) = mpsc::channel(10);
    
    let input = stream::iter(vec\![1, 2, 3, 4, 5]);
    forward(input, tx_out).await.unwrap();
    
    let output = ReceiverStream::new(rx_out);
    let collected: Vec<_> = output.collect().await;
    
    assert_eq\!(collected, vec\![1, 2, 3, 4, 5]);
}
```

### E2E Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn e2e_channel_stream_pipeline() {
    setup_test_logging();
    
    let rt = LabRuntime::new();
    rt.block_on(async {
        info\!("Starting channel stream pipeline E2E test");
        
        // Create pipeline: producer -> transform -> consumer
        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(100);
        let (tx_out, rx_out) = mpsc::channel(100);
        
        // Producer
        let producer = tokio::spawn(async move {
            for i in 1..=100 {
                tx.send(i).await.unwrap();
            }
            info\!("Producer done");
        });
        
        // Transform stage
        let transformer = tokio::spawn(async move {
            let stream = ReceiverStream::new(rx)
                .filter(|n| n % 2 == 0)
                .map(|n| n * n);
            
            forward(stream, tx_out).await.unwrap();
            info\!("Transformer done");
        });
        
        // Consumer
        let consumer = tokio::spawn(async move {
            let stream = ReceiverStream::new(rx_out);
            let results: Vec<_> = stream.collect().await;
            info\!(count = results.len(), "Consumer collected results");
            results
        });
        
        producer.await.unwrap();
        transformer.await.unwrap();
        let results = consumer.await.unwrap();
        
        // Should have 50 even numbers squared
        assert_eq\!(results.len(), 50);
        assert_eq\!(results[0], 4);  // 2^2
        assert_eq\!(results[49], 10000); // 100^2
        
        info\!("E2E test completed successfully");
    });
}
```
